review_title: Tool that anyone can pick up
author: Trinity M.
pros: You can actually create anything you desire with no restrictions by simply including things and moving it around. If you change your mind about an image you included to your page you can simply change it with a click, without having to resize and rearrange it to match the initial.
cons: The client assistance got a live chat service which is offline the majority of the time. rates is low-cost however you can discover comparable services for even less online.

review_title: Best platform to create your website
description: I built my business with Pagecloud. After testing the most known platforms on the market, I have to admit that Pagecloud is the best by far.
author: Markus D.
pros: User friendly platform, no coding needed (but possible if you need), best customer service ever, replies usually within hours, very reasonable price for what you get
cons: Honestly, I haven't experienced any cons. If there is any issue or help needed, contact customer support and they are replying within hours.

review_title: Great product that simplifies web design for the average user.
description: I was able to build two websites from scratch in the matter of days! This was previously unheard of in my circle. I used to use different web apps to build web pages but after coming across an Ad on Facebook I was instantly thrilled to hear the option to design via Photoshop and then send it to Pagecloud. That was absolutely brilliant.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love the idea of being able to design my website via Photoshop then transfer those assets into a functioning website. It allows me the flexibility to create in a program that I am familiar with and reduce the amount of coding / back end knowledge that you would otherwise need to create a website.
cons: The design mode is a bit convoluted in terms of keeping track of layers. If there were a panel that allows to you track layers similar to Photoshop that would make designing a whole lot easier. I wish there were more templates to choose from. A huge roadblock I have encountered is transferring your desktop version to the mobile version when it comes the sizing of some objects. That has been a frustrating process for me. As well as the lack of being able to have a background video automatically play for phone browsers.

review_title: Stands up to the Competition, and then some ...
description: Amazing!! Again, the support and development teams are phenomenal. I just love them. Other than the product itself, they are a huge reason I've stuck around to see where they take it next. :)
author: Jesslyn B.
pros: I've been with PageCloud almost from the beginning, and the strides they've made with improving their product to be more competitive with other options is truly noticeable. I can NOT say enough about their support team. Truly a few notches about the rest. Always so kind, extremely responsive, and they take care of you when you're needing help with something. I've never had a bad interaction with them and have always gotten a straight answer/resolution. Likewise, the development team actually listens to their customer feedback. I know that implementing new features takes a lot of dedicated time and work, but they've never failed to continually progress in getting much needed features from beta into live-use. Just to name a few of my favorite and most-used features: - Desktop view vs Mobile view, and being able to tweak each one separately - Being able to access the code and work with that - Drag and drop ease (obviously!) - Sections and how easy it is to copy, remove, adjust, and rearrange them - Etc. etc. ... I could go on and on :)
cons: The blog feature still leaves much to be desired, though I know that this is one of the next big updates, so I'm very much looking forward to that. I love that it is easy to organize and search, using keywords, etc., but the lack of customization on how it looks (formatting, typeface, etc.) is so far askew from the rest of PageCloud's immense customizability that I haven't used it other than quick tests on the backend. Once I'm able to dial that in, though, I very much look forward to using it to its full potential.

review_title: A Walk in the Park
description: We were able to create a very polished website using the flexible and adaptable templates available from Pagecloud. As a volunteer run organization, the cost, hosting, security, site upgrades and maintenance were critical considerations in our decision to choose Pagecloud. We really appreciated the excellent support.
author: Kerri W.
pros: The software was intuitive and the user support was prompt and friendly. The free trial period allowed us to explore the tools and templates to assess whether Pagecloud would work for our organization's needs. It is easy to continue to develop the content of the site without having to go to an outside service.
cons: Site wide styles for text needs improving. Rudimentary blog ability. Content formatting box: As a graphic designer I find the text is awkward in its hierarchy of information and the information display is tiny.

review_title: PC is so easy to use and VERY user friendly :) I tell EVERYONE I know that they need to us PC
description: I am able to create and easily maintain my own website, without having to hire someone or contact someone every time I need updates done.
author: Sarah Lynn S.
pros: I love the layout and features. The menu is simple and easy on the eyes. I like that all the main things you need are in the little menu that comes up with each box, like when I ad a picture, all the editing choices I might want are right there and then when I click on another area, it edits just that.... Its really a great software :)
cons: Sometimes it can be hard to un-group something and it ends up editing the wrong stop, but its super easy to fix with a simple ctrl+z ....Overall I really don't have anything I don't like about the software . Although it would be nice if it was a bit easier to add a new font, but that's about it.

review_title: page cloud allows a novice with no web design experience to create basically anything you can dream
description: I have created a strong, functional, lead capturing web site for real estate. I was also able to create a back end for my team and created an online training academy for my employees.
author: J. Alexander C.
pros: ease of use. create a blank page, drag virtually anything you want (picture, file to download, text) onto the page and it instantly becomes part of your website. Page cloud integrates with tons of free programs (and guides you to them through their training pages), so you can learn , grow, and improve your site at your own pace
cons: the integration from the desktop site to the mobile site is tricky. if you want a dedicated mobile page to go with your desktop page, as you change one the other one changes and becomes arduous to manage. I realized eventually that with todays smartphones and tablets I could eliminate the mobile pages , but i've still found that what shows up on the back end is frequently not fully compatible with I-phones

review_title: Breath of fresh air
description: I first heard about the site from a friend because our website was getting hacked quite often and Rogers, where we hosted before, could not really do anything for us to stop the hacking, just saying, hire a web designer. As I have always liked doing my own website for ease of uploading changes and customization to our business (training workshops for mental health professionals), I needed to find another service that would provide that ease of use with top notch security. Pagecloud, with their security partner Cloudfare, has done this. We have had zero security issues since switching. And the site looks a lot better too. I also like how easy it is to integrate other apps, like Jotforms and Paypal buttons.
author: Brian T.
pros: I liked the fact that I did not need to know coding or html, just basic desktop design skills, so very easy to get started. Also liked that it came with secure server pages, didn't need to find that elsewhere.
cons: I started using it 2 years ago and there were issues with setting up a nav bar easily on every page, it was a laborious process and not seamless. But this has now been corrected with a simple button for nav bars on every page. They have continuously improved the interface and things keep getting better.

review_title: The future of the web and of customer service
description: I spent months trying to create my own webpage without any coding experience and was never satisfied with the options available on the market. When I first signed up for PageCloud (before they had even released a product) I was very skeptical of their claims, because it seemed that if something like this were to be possible it would already exist. They delivered on their promises big time and have continued to improve and update rapidly. I'm very excited to see what they are able to do from here!
author: Warren C.
pros: Using PageCloud finally allowed me to unleash my creative ideas for my businesses' and my personal webpage. There are no boxes that I am forced to fit into, the interface is akin to Photoshop but with slightly more ease of use, and almost anything that I cannot do within the PageCloud domain I am able to find through their integratabtle Apps section. However, their strongest feature is their dedicated customer service team. This company is a model of how a customer service should function.
cons: I still am not able to have locked or password protected pages through PageCloud.

review_title: Pagecloud is an incredibly easy web design tool. Ease of use and functionality are off the charts!
description: Using PageCloud has helped me realize there are more ways to be creative with a website. Also, this has spurred me on to get additional training to help make my sites more useful and interactive.
author: Kit B.
pros: The ease of dropping in maps, videos, forms and (most importantly) FONTS - make this a fantastic tool for web designers who know nothing about coding AND designers who are very well versed in HTML and CSS. The customer service is always fast and friendly. Over the last couple of years I have submitted hard and easy questions for customer support, and am answered quickly and with no judgement!
cons: I do not like how there is no way to siphon pages into logical folders (unless there IS, and I just do not know about it). For instance, if I was creating a sporting goods site, or a sports news site, and I had pages with content (or items for sale) based on different sports... I wouldn't want just 300 pages on the root level of the site - I would want to be able to organize them into logical folders (like Winter Sports/Summer Sports or Baseball, Football, Hockey, etc). Also, I think the price is a wee bit high. Luckly, I am grandfathered in at the early adopter rate. And that is for my own web site. I would love to get another account for some of my clients, but they would not want to pay the monthly fee charged. If, as an early adopter I could get multiple sites for the special rate, I would load up. If you ever decided to do that - please contact me immediately!

review_title: The perfect website builder to release your creativity
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love it because basically it's a white paper - you can do whatever you want to do, totally open minded, no frames. Templates are also available but if you want to do your own thing without having programing knowledge, this is the way to go. Similar softwares where you can build your website too have always restrictions and you can't do it exactly the way you want it. In PageCloud I have no restrictions. And in case I need something which needs programing knowledge to implement a code, I contact PageClouds customer service and they are super helpful with instructing you they way you have to do it. When you have programing knowledge you can even implement codes yourself. Overall after trying several softwares in the past and being with PageCloud from day 1 with several business and private websites I can highly recommend PageCloud as being my number 1 website builder.
cons: Honestly, there is nothing bad I can write about it. When I have an issue I contact PageCloud customer service and I usually get an answer/solution within hours and that's all the time like this within the past 2 years. Their service is really outstanding, super friendly people, very personal service and I always get an answer on my questions and can continue with my work, it's just a really great service.

review_title: Honestly deserves 5-stars all around!
description: Building websites with PageCloud is easier and more advanced than the competitor.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Sometimes it hard to believe a review with full customer satisfaction, but honestly, PageCloud delivers. I have been a user since it launched a couple years ago. I was a fan back then and I am an even bigger fan with the new improvements. PageCloud is perfect for someone who has little/basic coding knowledge, or who can at least copy and paste intelligently. From a design standpoint, I do everything in Photoshop (which I am very comfortable in) and then transfer to PageCloud. The learning curve isn't terrible and the advanced features for a drag-and-drop design interface are top notch compared to the more entry level competitor sites such as Wix. If you want a professional site and you're not a huge fan of black and white coding, PageCloud is the go-to.
cons: There are very few things I dislike. I think they are constantly improving and listening to customer feedback, so that's definitely worth something. Personally, I would never build a site on anything else.

review_title: Changed to Pagecloud. Can't imagine ever going back to old methods.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This makes designing and building websites a breeze. A true "drag & drop" platform that gives you granular control over everything. I used many other types of website platforms like Joomla and Wordpress in the past. I would dig through tons of templates having to install each one then load the page. Then, I would install countless plugins and add-ons trying to ensure my page would behave how I wanted. Once you try it, you will never go back.
cons: There is a learning curve like any new thing. Trying to remember shortcuts, and figuring out what options do what. Unlike other web hosting packages, email is not included. You do get 1 year of Gmail suite free, however.

review_title: Great concept, Fantastic UX, Limited API and 3PA ability.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Pageclouds ability to clone an existing webpage is one of the biggest draws, it allows you to take an existing concept that you like and use it as a baseline for your project.
cons: The lack of third-party applications and integrations made it difficult to create fully featured pages without excessive coding.

review_title: Good quality but very limiting
description: Ease of use and easy to learn.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Drag and Drop feature makes work flow easy and fast for editing. Easy learning curve and familiarity with other products.
cons: Not enough choices in the features provided. I would like to see more form types and database implementation. I would like to be able to save the customer's form inputs and display products based on the type of car, for example. Currently, we only have contact form and newsletter forms.

review_title: Easy w/ Great Support
description: I am using pagecloud exclusively for all existing and new restaurant clients. It's easy for them to learn and edit sheet I'm done and ther templates work well in that industry. Great CS, the live chat feature is extremely helpful.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy for my clients to use secret I handle the initial build. I can train, even ther most un-savvy client to exit in pagecloud in about 30 min.
cons: It would be great to be able to upload more than just images.

review_title: Compared to Webbly and the like, PageCloud feels like cheating.
description: PageCloud allows me to quickly publish material in almost exactly the way I desire and without dealing with code. It frees me to focus on my writing and avoid spinning my wheels troubleshooting website basics.
author: Daniel M.
pros: I can do almost anything I want with text and boxes without having to switch between sections in an interface or troubleshoot code. Dragging and dropping items, resizing text, and a sufficient and well-thought-out toolbox on an interface that almost always matches what comes out in the browser is something I can't do without now.
cons: As the software is in development, some features, in particular creating links simplified and lost a feature or two. For example, I created links that opened in a new window without the browser features. I wasn't able to do this after the new version came out. There were some other features, such as creating a hamburger menu that didn't seem to work with the same ease.

review_title: PageCloud Awesome Experience
description: The overall experience was awesome. Like I said, I was doing a Golf Course Website and when I had questions they make sure that the customer service agent was passionate about Golf. I unfortunately did not finish completely the website as the whole golf course project went away but I'll definitely reuse PageCloud for another project.
author: Guillaume L.
pros: Customer service was perfect. Even thought sometimes I was speaking to different agent I always add a good service. I was doing a Golf course website and when I had questions they even give me a customer service agent that was passionate about Golf. They really care. I also liked the ease of use of the platform and the feeling using it.
cons: Honestly I don't see big cons about this service. I have to say that I did not try lots of competitors.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: PageCloud Gives You Flexibility That Other Platforms Don't Provide
description: PageCloud is genuinely easy to use. Your imagination is nearly the only limitation when designing a website. PageCloud's customer support is second to none.
author: Josh J.
pros: Flexibly. The fluid layout and ability to transition between full screen layout and mobile layout significantly cuts the time required to do work on the design instead of having to recreate a version of a page that is mobile friendly.
cons: Lack of database features, ability to create subdomains compared to other products within similar price range, lack of ability to create a login for a page (like a subscription page) to restrict content to certain people or groups.

review_title: I signed up to Pagecloud before it had launched and have been more than satisfied with the platform!
description: Ease of use and easy delivery of my website!
author: Eb A.
pros: It enables me to maintain my website in a very user friendly way. The tech support has been excellent and the platform is always improving!
cons: There is very little lacking from the platform and many features that I have wanted are coming online. One feature that would be excellent would be the ability to edit on my iPad or even iPhone!

review_title: The Best Drag and Drop Website Builder
description: My experience with PageCloud is really awesome, because I have created a lot of my websites through PageCloud. I really like the drag & drop features, because now I can create any website within few clicks.
author: Umair B.
pros: PageCloud is the best platform that allows creator to create, build and design websites. You can use existing manual layout to create beautiful and stunning layouts. You can also access search engine optimization tools for your website. You can build websites for every screen including mobile and desktop devices.
cons: The interface of PageCloud is really easy to understand and use, even a beginner can design and build the website. So according to my experience, there are no cons.

review_title: Intriguing Platform of the Future
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I love about PageCloud is the ease of use. The fact that you can drag and drop and create beautiful websites in minutes instead of hours or days is incredible. As a designer, it allows me to work as fast as my imagination flows, which makes rapid prototyping and developing iterative designs so easy.
cons: While the technology is incredible, the platform is still very young. There are features that are not available and workarounds that still need to be done. While no platform will ever be perfect, PageCloud is extremely promising and one that I will continue to watch.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great team to work with. My questions are answered almost immediately. Highly recommend!
author: Greg W.
pros: Ease of use and the support team are awesome!! I have been with them from the beginning, the product just continues to get better and the features they keep adding are awesome. Can't wait to see what they come up with next.
cons: I don't think I could say anything bad about the software to this point. I have been a customer for 2 years now and they have been a great company to work with.

review_title: Love it
author: Ismail B.
pros: Complete freedom in terms of design. You know for a fact that anything you make and publish on PageCloud will be completely unique which feels great. Anything that you picture in your head you can create on PageCloud.
cons: Tiny bugs here and there (Hard time dragging objects occasionally). But nothing major at all.

review_title: I love the ability to use my website like a canvas and update with relative ease.
author: Christine H.
pros: What I like most about the software is it's flexibility and intuitive nature. I like being able to change design elements at will. I also appreciate the tutorials and the prompt customer service that PageCloud offers.
cons: What I like lease about this software is that it doesn't yet have all the design options I would like to implement
